title: Orbital Mechanics — SuperBEST v3 Cost Analysis

equations.name: Orbital velocity
equations.folding_note: GM treated as single free constant

equations.name: Orbital period (Kepler's third law)
equations.folding_note: Absorb 2*pi/sqrt(GM) into one constant; use pow(a,1.5) directly

equations.name: Hohmann transfer delta-V1
equations.unfolded_breakdown: v_circ=sqrt(GM/r1): div(GM,r1)=1+pow=3=4; add(r1,r2)=3; mul(2,r2)=2; div(2r2,sum)=1; pow(0.5)=3; sub(sqrt,1)=2; mul(v_circ,result)=2 => 4+3+2+1+3+2+2=17
equations.folded_breakdown: GM constant saves 2n on v_circ (4n), rest unchanged: 4+3+2+1+3+2+2=17
equations.folding_note: GM constant already counted in unfolded; minimal folding opportunity here beyond GM

equations.name: Gravitational lensing deflection angle
equations.folded_breakdown: 4G as constant=0, mul(4G,M)=2, pow(c,2)=3, mul(c2,b)=2, div=1
equations.folding_note: 4G absorbed into one constant; c could also be constant giving further reduction

equations.name: Roche limit
equations.folding_note: When rho_M and rho_m are fixed physical parameters, their ratio is a free constant

equations.name: Hill sphere radius
equations.folded_breakdown: m/(3M) as compound constant=0, pow(const,1/3)=3, mul(a,result)=2
equations.folding_note: When m and M are fixed masses, m/(3M) is a free constant

equations.name: Synodic period
equations.folded_breakdown: No folding opportunity; all four operations are structurally necessary
equations.folding_note: Each recip and the sub are essential; no constants absorb the variable structure

notes: SuperBEST v3 cost schema: exp=1, ln=1, neg=2, recip=2, mul=2, sub=2, div=1, pow=3, add(pos)=3; parameters/constants are free (cost 0).
Folded costs assume physical constants (G, M, c, 2pi, density ratios) can be collapsed into single free terminals when they are fixed for a given problem instance.
Hohmann dV1 has no folding savings beyond the GM constant already reflected in the unfolded count; its branching structure (two square roots, one subtraction, one product) is irreducible.
Synodic period has no folding savings; the reciprocal-subtract-reciprocal chain is structurally unavoidable.
pow(x, 1/3) and pow(x, 3/2) each cost 3n regardless of whether the exponent is rational or irrational — the exponent is a free constant.
